scopes.name: _animation
scopes.desc: A namespace to control animations and sequence animations.

functions.name: createAnimation
functions.desc: Create an instance of an animation from a definition.

params.label: String
params.desc: The name of the animation definition from which this one should be created.

params.label: AnimationInfo
params.desc: Information the animation needs to complete. Created by createAnimationInfo().

functions.returns: An instance of an animation created from the specified animation definiton.

functions.name: createAnimationInfo
functions.desc: Create an animation info object from a list of objects. This info is used to construct an animation.

params.label: array
params.desc: An array with max size 16 containing animatable objects.

functions.returns: An animation info instance.

functions.name: loadAnimationFile
functions.desc: Load and parse animations from an animation file

params.label: String
params.desc: A res path to an animation file.

functions.name: getNumActiveAnimations
functions.desc: Get the number of active animations currently run by the animation manager.
functions.returns: An integer of the number of animations.

scopes.name: _audio
scopes.desc: A namespace to control audio.

functions.name: play
functions.desc: Play audio output

functions.name: pause
functions.desc: Pause audio output

functions.name: newSource
functions.desc: Create a new audio source.

functions.name: newSourceFromBuffer
functions.desc: Create a new sound buffer, passing in a pre-existing buffer.

functions.name: newSoundBuffer
functions.desc: Create a new sound buffer. This buffer can be re-used to create many audio sources.

functions.name: setListenerPosition
functions.desc: Set the position of the listener

functions.name: getListenerPosition
functions.desc: Get the position of the listener

functions.name: getListenerVelocity
functions.desc: Get the velocity of the listener
functions.returns: A Vec3 containing the velocity.

functions.name: setListenerVelocity
functions.desc: Set velocity for the listener.

params.label: Vec3
params.desc: Vector3 containing the velocity.

functions.name: setListenerOrientation
functions.desc: Set the orientation of the listener.

params.label: Quaternion
params.desc: Orientation of the listener.

functions.name: setVolume
functions.desc: Set the master volume.

params.label: float
params.desc: Float decimal representing volume.

functions.name: getVolume
functions.desc: Get the master volume.
functions.returns: Volume as a float decimal.

functions.name: getNumAudioSources
functions.desc: Get the number of audio sources which currently exist..

functions.name: getNumAudioBuffers
functions.desc: Get the number of audio buffers which currently exist..

scopes.name: _camera
scopes.desc: Namespace to alter the camera.

functions.name: setPosition
functions.desc: Set the position of the camera.

params.label: x
params.desc: x position.

params.label: y
params.desc: y position.

params.label: z
params.desc: x position.

overloads.desc: Set the position of the camera.

params.label: Vec3
params.desc: Vector representing the position in 3d space.

overloads.desc: Set the position of the camera.

params.label: SlotPosition
params.desc: SlotPosition representing the position in world coordinates. This will be resolved to local coordinates automatically.

functions.name: getPosition
functions.desc: Get the position of the camera as a vector3.

functions.name: getOrientation
functions.desc: Get the orientation of the camera as a quaternion.

functions.name: lookAt
functions.desc: Point the camera towards a specific coordinate.

params.label: x
params.desc: Point x position.

params.label: y
params.desc: Point y position.

params.label: z
params.desc: Point x position.

overloads.desc: Point the camera towards a specific coordinate.

params.label: Vec3
params.desc: Vector representing the position to look at.

overloads.desc: Point the camera towards a specific coordinate.

params.label: SlotPosition
params.desc: SlotPosition representing the position in world coordinates. This will be resolved to local coordinates automatically.

functions.name: setOrientation
functions.desc: Set the orientation of the camera.

params.label: Quaternion
params.desc: The quaternion orientation to set.

functions.name: getCameraToViewportRay
functions.desc: Obtain a ray pointing in a specific direction based on coordinates.

params.label: y
params.desc: A normalised float value for the y coordinates.

functions.name: getCamera
functions.desc: Get the camera as an object. With this it can be used as part of compositor effects.
functions.returns: A camera object

functions.name: setDirection
functions.desc: Set the direction the camera faces.

params.label: Vector3
params.desc: A direction vector.

functions.name: getDirection
functions.desc: Get the direction the camera faces.
functions.returns: A normalised direction vector.

functions.name: setProjectionType
functions.desc: Set the projection type to use for this camera.

functions.name: setOrthoWindow
functions.desc: Sets the orthographic window settings, for use with orthographic rendering only.

functions.name: setAspectRatio
functions.desc: set the aspect ration of the camera. Generally will be width / height.

functions.name: setFOVy
functions.desc: Set the vertical field of view of the camera, in radians.

scopes.name: _compositor
scopes.desc: Namespace to perform compositor tasks.

functions.name: addWorkspace
functions.desc: Add a workspace based on a loaded definition.

params.label: Array
params.desc: An array containing the renderable inputs and output to give to the workspace.

params.label: Camera
params.desc: The camera to use for the workspace.

params.label: bool
params.desc: Whether or not this workspace is automatically enabled.

functions.name: removeWorkspace
functions.desc: Remove a workspace. This will invalidate it if any references exist.

params.label: CompositorWorkspace
params.desc: The workspace to remove.

scopes.name: _developer
scopes.desc: Functionality specifically for developer purposes. This includes things such as drawing meshes, lines, and text. This namespace is only available if DEBUGGING_TOOLS is enabled.

functions.name: setMeshGroupVisible
functions.desc: Change the visibility of one of the mesh type catagories.

params.label: meshType
params.desc: The mesh type to make visible. 1 - physics chunks.

params.label: visible
params.desc: A boolean of whether or not this shape should be visible.

functions.name: setRenderQueueForMeshGroup
functions.desc: Change the render queue of all objects in the mesh visualiser.

params.label: integer
params.desc: The render queue value between 0 and 100.

functions.name: drawPoint
functions.desc: Draw a debug point in 3D space. This function should be called each frame the point should be drawn.

params.label: SlotPosition
params.desc: The position where the point should be drawn.

functions.name: drawAxis
functions.desc: Draw a straight line from a point representing an axis.

params.label: SlotPosition
params.desc: The position where the axis should be drawn.

params.label: Axis
params.desc: A number representing the axis. 0 is x, 1 is y, 2 is z. Anything else will draw x.

functions.name: drawCircle
functions.desc: Draw a single circle at a position, with a specified radius, which faces up on the Y axis.

params.label: SlotPosition
params.desc: The position where the circle should be drawn.

params.label: Radius
params.desc: A float or int representing the drawn circle radius.

functions.name: drawSphere
functions.desc: Draw a sphere comprised of lines, with a specified radius.

params.label: SlotPosition
params.desc: The position where the sphere should be drawn.

params.label: Radius
params.desc: A float or int representing the drawn sphere radius.

functions.name: unblock
functions.desc: Unblock the execution of the dialog.

functions.name: compileAndRunDialog
functions.desc:
Compile a dialog script and begin execution. This does not create any sort of reusable reference to the compiled dialog. It's more there for convenience.
This function will start with block 0. If this block is not present nothing will be run.

params.label: path
params.desc: A res path to a dialog script file.

functions.name: compileDialog
functions.desc: Compile a dialog script and return it. This function does not execute the dialog.

params.label: path
params.desc: A res path to a dilog script file.

functions.returns: A compiled dialog script.

functions.name: executeCompiledDialog
functions.desc: Begin execution of a compiled dialog.

params.label: compiledDialog
params.desc: A dialog compiled by the compileDialog function.

params.label: blockId
params.desc: An integer representing a block to begin execution with. Defaults to 0.

functions.name: update
functions.desc: Perform an update of the dialog system. Update is called by the engine each frame. This function is really just a utility function, useful for testing.

functions.name: isDialogExecuting
functions.returns: True or false depending on whether the dialog is currently executing.

overloads.returns: True or false depending on whether the dialog is currently blocked.

functions.name: specifyOption
functions.desc:
Specify an option as part of the option tag.
This function can only be called while the dialog is blocked on an option, otherwise an error will be thrown.
If succesful, this function will unblock the execution.

scopes.name: _entity
scopes.desc: Function to interact with the entity system. Note that many of these functions will fail if the world does not exist.

functions.name: create
functions.desc: Create an untracked entity.

params.label: slotPosition
params.desc: The position to create the entity at.

functions.returns: A handle to the created entity.

functions.name: createTracked
functions.desc: Create a tracked entity.

params.label: slotPosition
params.desc: The position to create the entity at.

functions.returns: A handle to the created entity.

functions.name: destroy
functions.desc: Destroy an entity

params.label: entityHandle
params.desc: The entity to destroy.

overloads.desc: Set an entity to be tracked.

params.label: entityHandle
params.desc: The entity to be tracked.

functions.name: untrack
functions.desc: Set an entity to be untracked.

params.label: entityHandle
params.desc: The entity to be untracked.

scopes.name: _event
scopes.desc: Namespace for registering functions to events

functions.name: subscribe
functions.desc: Subscribe a closure to an event. The provided closure will be called when the event occurs. As well as this, the user is able to provide a context in which the closure will be called.

params.label: EventId
params.desc: The id of the target event. If the value is greater than 1000 it will be registered as a user event.

params.label: Closure
params.desc: The closure which should be called.

functions.name: unsubscribe
functions.desc: Unsubscribe from an event.

params.label: EventId
params.desc: The id of the target event. For this version, the id must be a system id, meaning it must be less than 1000.

overloads.desc: Unsubscribe a closure to an event. If no closure is provided an error will be thrown.

params.label: EventId
params.desc: The id of the target event. If the value is greater than 1000 this is assumed to be a user event.

params.label: Closure
params.desc: If this is a user event, the target closure must be provided.

functions.name: transmit
functions.desc: Subscribe a closure to an event. The provided closure will be called when the event occurs. As well as this, the user is able to provide a context in which the closure will be called.

params.label: EventId
params.desc: A user event id. This value must be greater than 1000 or an error will be thrown.

params.label: Data
params.desc: A generic data object which is sent as part of the subscription.

scopes.name: _graphics
scopes.desc: Exposes functions to access graphical functions, for instance textures.

functions.name: createTexture
functions.desc: Create a texture by name. Throws an error if that texture already exists.

params.label: String
params.desc: The name of the texture to create.

functions.name: getStagingTexture
functions.desc: Get a staging texture, which can be used to upload texture data from the CPU to GPU.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: width

params.label: Integer
params.desc: height

params.label: Integer
params.desc: depth

params.label: Integer
params.desc: slices

params.label: PixelFormat
params.desc: Pixel format

functions.name: createOrRetreiveTexture
functions.desc: Create a texture by name, unless it exists in which case return it.

params.label: String
params.desc: The name of the texture.

functions.name: destroyTexture
functions.desc: Destroy a texture by handle. Must be a user editable texture or an error will be thrown.

params.label: String
params.desc: The name of the texture to create.

functions.name: getLoadedTextures
functions.desc: Returns a list of every texture which is currently registered to the texture manager.

functions.name: getGpuProgramByName
functions.desc: Obtain a handle to a GPUProgram by its name.

params.label: String
params.desc: The name identifier of the gpu program.

functions.returns: :The found GPU program. An error is thrown if nothing is found.

functions.name: createManualMesh
functions.desc: Create a mesh object to be populated through code, i.e not loaded from a mesh file.

params.label: String
params.desc: The resource name

params.label: String
params.desc: The resource group

functions.returns: : A mesh object

functions.name: removeManualMesh
functions.desc: Remove a manual mesh created previously.

params.label: String
params.desc: The resource name

functions.name: createVertexBuffer
functions.desc: Create a vertex buffer object.

params.label: VertexElemVec
params.desc: VertexElemVec to describe the packing of the vertices.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: Number of vertices.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: Stride size. Ignored - Ogre derives the stride from the vertex elements.

params.label: DataBlob
params.desc: Blob of data containing the vertice data.

params.label: Boolean
params.desc: Whether to keep a CPU shadow copy of the data. Defaults to true. Pass false for buffers you intend to re-upload often.

functions.returns: : A populated vertex buffer.

functions.name: createIndexBuffer
functions.desc: Create an index buffer object.

params.label: IndexBufferType
params.desc: Define _IT_16BIT or _IT_32BIT bits for the buffer.

params.label: DataBlob
params.desc: Indice data.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: Num indices.

functions.returns: : A populated index buffer.

functions.name: createVertexElemVec
functions.desc: Create a vertex element vector object
functions.returns: : A vertexElementVec object.

functions.name: createVertexArrayObject
functions.desc: Create a vertex array object.
functions.returns: : A populated VertexArrayObject.

functions.name: genPerlinNoiseTexture
functions.desc: Generate a texture pre-populated with perlin noise.

params.label: String
params.desc: name of texture.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: width of texture.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: height of texture.

params.label: float
params.desc: Frequency of noise.

params.label: integer
params.desc: Depth of noise.

functions.returns: : A texture populated with perlin noise.

functions.name: getMaterialByName
functions.desc: Obtain a handle to a material by its name.

params.label: String
params.desc: The name identifier of the material.

functions.returns: :The found material. An error is thrown if nothing is found.

scopes.name: _gui
scopes.desc: A namespace to create and control gui elements.

functions.name: createWindow
functions.desc: Create a window.

params.label: String
params.desc: Window query id.

params.label: Window
params.desc: Parent window.

functions.returns: A window object.

functions.name: createLayoutLine
functions.desc: Create a layout line object.

params.label: alignment
params.desc: An iterator specifying whether this is a horizontal or vertical layout line.

functions.returns: A layout line object.

functions.name: createLayoutTable
functions.desc: Create a layout table object.
functions.returns: A layout table object.

functions.name: destroy
functions.desc: Destroy a gui element.

params.label: GuiElement
params.desc: A widget, window or layout object.

functions.name: loadSkins
functions.desc: Load a GUI skin file.

params.label: String
params.desc: A res path to the target skin file.

functions.name: setDefaultFontSize26d6
functions.desc: Set the global default font size using the measuring system of 26d6. All created widgets will base themselves upon this.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: The new default font size.

functions.name: getDefaultFontSize26d6
functions.desc: Get the global default font size using the measuring system of 26d6.

functions.name: getOriginalDefaultFontSize26d6
functions.desc: Get the original default font size. This won't be effected by a change to the default size.

functions.name: setCanvasSize
functions.desc: Set the size of the canvas.

params.label: Vec2
params.desc: The size of the canvas in points.

params.label: Vec2
params.desc: The window resolution. Does not necessarily have to match the canvas size.

functions.name: setScrollSpeed
functions.desc: Set the speed at which the scroll wheel operates.

params.label: Vec2
params.desc: The size of the canvas in points.

functions.name: reprocessMousePosition
functions.desc: Force a re-process of the mouse position, without requiring a mouse move. This is useful if say a button press recently caused a complete change to a user interface.

functions.name: mapControllerInput
functions.desc: Map a controller input to a gui input.

functions.name: mapKeyboardInput
functions.desc: Map a keyboard input to a gui input.

functions.name: mapControllerAxis
functions.desc: Map a controller axis to four inputs.

functions.name: getMousePosGui
functions.desc: Get the mouse position relative to the gui system.

functions.name: simulateMouseButton
functions.desc: Simulate a mouse button press or release. Useful for automation and unique use cases.

functions.name: simulateMousePosition
functions.desc: Simulate a mouse move to the provided position.

functions.name: simulateGuiPrimary
functions.desc: Simulate a gui event on the primary key.

functions.name: getWindowForIdx
functions.desc: Return an active window for a specific index, allowing iteration of the windows.

scopes.name: _hlms
scopes.desc: This namespace provides mechanisms to interact with Ogre's HLMS (High Level Material System). Here, what are generally thought of as materials are named datablocks. Datablocks contain information relating to how objects should be rendered, so they can be thought of as materials.

functions.name: getDatablock
functions.desc: Retreives a pre-existing datablock from Ogre.

params.label: datablockName
params.desc: A string representing the datablock name.

functions.returns: A handle to a datablock. If the datablock was not found null will be returned.

functions.name: destroyDatablock
functions.desc: Destroys a pre-existing datablock.

params.label: datablock
params.desc: Either a string name, or a datablock handle.

functions.name: getMacroblock
functions.desc: Get a reference to a macroblock with some construction parameters.

params.label: table
params.desc: A table containing the construction info for that macroblock.

functions.name: getSamplerblock
functions.desc: Obtain a macroblock user data which can be used when altering datablocks through code.

params.label: table
params.desc: A table containing the construction info for that samplerblock.

functions.name: getBlendblock
functions.desc: Get a reference to a blendblock with some construction parameters.

params.label: table
params.desc: A table containing the construction info for that blendblock.

scopes.name: _input
scopes.desc: This namespace provides functionality to retreive input.

functions.name: getMouseX
functions.returns: The mouse x coordinate, relative to the top left of the window.

functions.name: getMouseY
functions.returns: The mouse y coordinate, relative to the top left of the window.

functions.name: getMouseWheelValue
functions.returns: Obtain the current delta values of the mouse wheel.

functions.name: getMouseButton

params.label: buttonId
params.desc: The id of the mouse button to retreive.

functions.returns: True or false depending on whether the mouse button is down.

functions.name: getMousePressed

params.label: buttonId
params.desc: The id of the mouse button to retreive.

functions.returns: True or false depending on whether the mouse button was pressed last frame.

functions.name: getMouseReleased

params.label: buttonId
params.desc: The id of the mouse button to retreive.

functions.returns: True or false depending on whether the mouse button was pressed last frame.

functions.name: getButtonActionHandle

params.label: handleName
params.desc: The name of the button action. Names must be unique across action sets.

functions.returns: A handle to a button action. This can later be used to retrieve input values.

functions.name: getAxisActionHandle

params.label: handleName
params.desc: The name of the axis action. Names must be unique across action sets.

functions.returns: A handle to an axis action.

functions.name: getTriggerActionHandle

params.label: handleName
params.desc: The name of the trigger action. Names must be unique across action sets.

functions.returns: A handle to an trigger action.

functions.name: setActionSetForDevice
functions.desc: Set the action set for a particular device.

params.label: deviceId
params.desc: An integer id for the target device. Controllers this will be a value between 0 and _MAX_INPUT_DEVICES. _KEYBOARD_INPUT_DEVICE can also be passed.

params.label: actionSetHandle
params.desc: An action set handle.

functions.name: setAxisDeadzone
functions.desc: Set the deadzone for a device's axises.

params.label: number
params.desc: Required deadzone value between 0.0 and 1.0

params.label: deviceId
params.desc: An integer id for the target device. Value must be between 0 and _MAX_INPUT_DEVICES. _ANY_INPUT_DEVICE will set the deadzone for all devices.

functions.name: setDefaultAxisDeadzone
functions.desc: Set the default deadzone for all devices. When a new device is added the default deadzone value will be used.

params.label: number
params.desc: Required deadzone value between 0.0 and 1.0

params.label: buttonActionHandle
params.desc: A button action handle. This has to be a button handle.

params.label: inputType
params.desc: Filter what sort of values are returned. Types are _INPUT_ANY, _INPUT_PRESSED, _INPUT_RELEASED.

params.label: deviceId
params.desc: The device id to query. If none is provided the default any device will be used.

params.label: triggerActionHandle
params.desc: A trigger action handle.

params.label: inputType
params.desc: Filter what sort of values are returned. Types are _INPUT_ANY, _INPUT_PRESSED, _INPUT_RELEASED.

params.label: deviceId
params.desc: The device id to query. If none is provided the default any device will be used.

params.label: axisActionHandle
params.desc: An axis action handle.

params.label: inputType
params.desc: Filter what sort of values are returned. Types are _INPUT_ANY, _INPUT_PRESSED, _INPUT_RELEASED.

params.label: deviceId
params.desc: The device id to query. If none is provided the default any device will be used.

params.label: axisActionHandle
params.desc: An axis action handle.

params.label: inputType
params.desc: Filter what sort of values are returned. Types are _INPUT_ANY, _INPUT_PRESSED, _INPUT_RELEASED.

params.label: deviceId
params.desc: The device id to query. If none is provided the default any device will be used.

functions.name: setActionSets
functions.desc: Set the action sets which can be queried.

params.label: actionSets
params.desc: A table containing the action sets.

functions.name: getActionSetNames
functions.returns: A list containing all available action sets by name.

functions.name: getActionSetHandle
functions.desc: Get a handle to an action set via name.

params.label: actionSetName
params.desc: A string representing an action set. The action set name must be unique.

functions.returns: A handle representing an action set.

functions.name: getActionNamesForSet
functions.desc: Get a list of action names for an action set.

params.label: actionSetHandle
params.desc: The target action set.

params.label: type
params.desc: An integer representing the action type to query. 0 is an axis, 1 is a trigger, 2 is a button.

functions.returns: A list of strings representing the actions.

functions.name: getNumControllers
functions.desc: Get the number of controllers currently connected.
functions.returns: An integer representing the current number of controllers.

functions.name: getDeviceName
functions.desc: Get the name of an attached input device.

params.label: deviceId
params.desc: The device to query.

functions.returns: A string representing the device name.

functions.name: getRawKeyScancodeInput
functions.desc: Get the boolean pressed value for a keyboard scancode. This circumvents the action system, and is generally intended for writing tools or developer functions.

params.label: int
params.desc: The scancode to query.

functions.returns: A boolean representing the key state.

functions.name: mapControllerInput
functions.desc: Map an input id to an action.

params.label: inputId
params.desc: The target input to map. These values are different depending on the action handle.

params.label: actionHandle
params.desc: The target action handle to be bound.

functions.name: mapKeyboardInput
functions.desc: Map a keyboard button to an action. This works for both button and trigger actions.

functions.name: mapKeyboardInputAxis
functions.desc: Map multiple keyboard buttons to an axis. For instance you might want to map the WASD keys to an axis.

params.label: posX
params.desc: The key to assign to the positive x axis

params.label: posY
params.desc: The key to assign to the positive y axis.

params.label: negX
params.desc: The key to assign to the negative x axis.

params.label: negY
params.desc: The key to assign to the negative y axis.

params.label: actionHandle
params.desc: The handle to bind to the axis.

functions.name: clearAllMapping
functions.desc: Clear all the handles mapped to either keyboard or controller inputs.

functions.name: getMostRecentDevice
functions.desc:
Get the device which was used last frame.
Can be useful to determine which devices are actually being used to interact with the project.
functions.returns:
The most recent device which was used.
If multiple last frame, this will be in the order keyboard, then controllers 0-MAX_INPUT_DEVICES.

functions.name: sendButtonAction
functions.desc: Send a button action to the input system. Useful for spoofing inputs, i.e in onscreen controls.

params.label: ActionHandle
params.desc: The handle to send to.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: DeviceId.

params.label: Boolean
params.desc: Whether the button was pressed.

functions.name: sendTriggerAction
functions.desc: Send a trigger action to the input system. Useful for spoofing inputs, i.e in onscreen controls.

params.label: ActionHandle
params.desc: The handle to send to.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: DeviceId.

params.label: Number
params.desc: The trigger value.

functions.name: sendAxisAction
functions.desc: Send an axis action to the input system.  Useful for spoofing inputs, i.e in onscreen controls.

params.label: ActionHandle
params.desc: The handle to send to.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: DeviceId.

params.label: Number
params.desc: The axis value to set.

params.label: Boolean
params.desc: The axis value to set. True for x and false for y.

functions.name: sendKeyboardKeyPress
functions.desc: Send keyboard keypress to the input system.  Useful for spoofing inputs, i.e in onscreen controls.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: Key id to send the event to.

params.label: Boolean
params.desc: Whether or not the key is pressed.

functions.name: getTouchPosition
functions.desc: Get the position of a touch with finger id. Details of touches occuring are delivered via system events.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: FingerId to respond to.

functions.name: rumbleInputDevice
functions.desc: Send a haptic rumble effect to the specified device. Only available for controllers.

params.label: Float
params.desc: Decimal value representing Low frequency rumble strength.

params.label: Float
params.desc: Decimal value representing High frequency rumble strength.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: Rumble time in ms.

scopes.name: _lottie
scopes.desc: A namespace to manage lottie animations

functions.name: createAnimation
functions.desc: Create an instance of a lottie animation.

params.label: ResPath
params.desc: ResPath to the lottie animation JSON file which should be used.

functions.returns: A lottie animation instance.

functions.name: createSurface
functions.desc: Create an instance of a lottie surface.

params.label: height
params.desc: Height of the target surface.

functions.returns: A lottie surface

scopes.name: _mesh
scopes.desc: Functions to create meshes.

functions.name: create
functions.desc: Create a mesh.
functions.returns: a mesh instance.

functions.name: setBannerAdUnitId
functions.desc: Set the ad unit ID for banner ads. Must be called before showBannerAd.

params.label: unitId
params.desc: string The AdMob ad unit ID (e.g. "ca-app-pub-xxx/yyy")

functions.name: showBannerAd
functions.desc: Request and display a banner ad at the bottom of the screen.

functions.name: hideBannerAd
functions.desc: Hide the currently displayed banner ad.

functions.name: setInterstitialAdUnitId
functions.desc: Set the ad unit ID for interstitial (fullscreen) ads. Must be called before loadInterstitialAd.

params.label: unitId
params.desc: string The AdMob ad unit ID (e.g. "ca-app-pub-xxx/zzz")

functions.name: loadInterstitialAd
functions.desc: Begin loading an interstitial ad. Subscribe to _EVENT_ADVERTISING_INTERSTITIAL_LOADED to know when ready.

functions.name: showInterstitialAd
functions.desc: Present the loaded interstitial ad. Has no effect if no ad is ready.

functions.name: isInterstitialAdReady
functions.desc: Returns true if an interstitial ad has been loaded and is ready to display.
functions.returns: bool

functions.name: getBannerAdBounds
functions.desc: Get the bounds of the banner ad in physical screen pixels.
functions.returns: table with keys: x, y, width, height, active

functions.name: setPersonalisedAds
functions.desc: Enable or disable personalised ads. When disabled, requests non-personalised ads from Google Mobile Ads. Default is true. Applies to future ad requests.

params.label: enabled
params.desc: bool True to enable personalised ads, false for non-personalised ads only

functions.name: isPersonalisedAds
functions.desc: Returns whether personalised ads are currently enabled.
functions.returns: bool

functions.name: setRewardedAdUnitId
functions.desc: Set the ad unit ID for rewarded ads. Must be called before loadRewardedAd.

params.label: unitId
params.desc: string The AdMob ad unit ID (e.g. "ca-app-pub-xxx/zzz")

functions.name: loadRewardedAd
functions.desc: Begin loading a rewarded ad. Subscribe to _EVENT_ADVERTISING_REWARDED_LOADED to know when ready.

functions.name: showRewardedAd
functions.desc: Present the loaded rewarded ad. Has no effect if no ad is ready. Subscribe to _EVENT_ADVERTISING_REWARD_EARNED for reward verification and _EVENT_ADVERTISING_REWARDED_CLOSED for dismissal.

functions.name: isRewardedAdReady
functions.desc: Returns true if a rewarded ad has been loaded and is ready to display.
functions.returns: bool

functions.name: purchaseProduct
functions.desc: Initiate a purchase for the given product ID. Subscribe to _EVENT_PURCHASE_PRODUCT_PURCHASED or _EVENT_PURCHASE_PRODUCT_FAILED for the result.

params.label: productId
params.desc: string The platform product identifier (e.g. "com.example.coins100")

functions.name: restorePurchases
functions.desc: Restore previously completed purchases. Fires _EVENT_PURCHASE_PRODUCT_PURCHASED per product, then _EVENT_PURCHASE_RESTORE_COMPLETED or _EVENT_PURCHASE_RESTORE_FAILED.

functions.name: queryProductInfo
functions.desc: Request localised product info for the given product ID. Subscribe to _EVENT_PURCHASE_PRODUCT_INFO for the result.

params.label: productId
params.desc: string The platform product identifier

functions.name: canMakePayments
functions.desc: Returns true if the platform purchase manager is available and payments can be made.
functions.returns: bool

scopes.name: _nav
scopes.desc: Namespace to obtain information on the current nav meshes.

functions.name: getNumMeshes
functions.returns: The number of meshes currently loaded.

functions.name: getNumQueries
functions.returns: The number of active queries.

functions.name: getMeshByName
functions.desc: Retreive a mesh object by name.

params.label: String
params.desc: The name of the mesh.

functions.returns: A handle to the requested mesh. If the mesh does not exist an invalid mesh handle is returned.

functions.name: createQuery
functions.desc: Create a nav mesh query for a certain mesh. As an object this query can be re-used as many times as needed.

params.label: NavMesh
params.desc: The nav mesh to base the query on.

functions.returns: A nav mesh query instance.

scopes.name: _physics
scopes.desc: Functions to do things with physics.

functions.name: getCubeShape
functions.desc: Get a cube shape instance (bullet box shape).

params.label: x
params.desc: Half extends x

params.label: y
params.desc: Half extends y

params.label: z
params.desc: Half extends z

functions.returns: An instance of a cube shape.

functions.name: getSphereShape
functions.desc: Get a sphere shape instance.

params.label: radius
params.desc: Sphere radius

functions.returns: An instance of a sphere shape

functions.name: getCapsuleShape
functions.desc: Get a capsule shape instance.
functions.returns: An instance of a sphere shape

functions.name: setCollisionCallbackOverride
functions.desc: Override the function which the collision world calls on collision. When this function is set, no other functions will be called by the collision manager. This is useful for building editor tools or inspectors.

params.label: func
params.desc: A closure which should be called when a collision occurs. If null is provided the override will be disabled.

scopes.name: _random
scopes.desc: A utility namespace to generate random numbers.

functions.name: rand
functions.desc: Generate a random number between 0 and 1.
functions.returns: A float between 0 and 1.

functions.name: randInt
functions.desc: Generate a random integer between two bounds. A single input can be provided, which is used as the upper bound, while the lower bound is assumed to be 0.

params.desc: :lowerBound
:upperBound

functions.name: randVec3
functions.desc: Generate Vector3 containing random numbers between 0 and 1.

functions.name: randVec2
functions.desc: Generate Vector2 containing random numbers between 0 and 1.

functions.name: randAABB
functions.desc: Generate a Vector3 position in an AABB.

params.label: AABB
params.desc: aabb The AABB to generate the random point from.

params.label: Bool
params.desc: World Whether to apply the aabb in world space or local.

functions.name: randIndex
functions.desc: Generate a random index for for a provided array.

params.label: array
params.desc: An array to generate an index for. Throws an error if the array has length of 0.

functions.name: genPerlinNoise
functions.desc: Generate a set of perlin noise data of specified size.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: Width of noise to generate.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: Height of noise to generate.

params.label: float
params.desc: Frequency of noise.

params.label: integer
params.desc: Depth of noise.

functions.name: seedPatternGenerator
functions.desc: Seed the pattern generator with a value.

params.label: integer
params.desc: seed value to use.

functions.name: seed
functions.desc: Seed the random number generator.

params.label: integer
params.desc: seed value to use.

scopes.name: _registry
scopes.desc: The registry provides a gloabal method for the storage and retreival of arbuitrary values. These values can be read in scripts, as well as in the dialog system. It is intended for values which should last a long time, i.e across saves. It's useful for things like how much money the player has, what their score is, etc.

functions.name: getInt
functions.desc: Returns an integer value based on a key name.

params.label: valName
params.desc: The name of the value to retreived.

functions.returns: An integer. If either the value is not found, or the found value was not an integer, this function returns null.

functions.name: getFloat
functions.desc: Returns a float value based on a key name.

params.label: valName
params.desc: The name of the value to retreived.

functions.returns: A float. If either the value is not found, or the found value was not a float, this function returns null.

functions.name: getBool
functions.desc: Returns a boolean value based on a key name.

params.label: valName
params.desc: The name of the value to retreived.

functions.returns: A boolean. If either the value is not found, or the found value was not a boolean, this function returns null.

functions.name: getString
functions.desc: Returns a string value based on a key name.

params.label: valName
params.desc: The name of the value to retreived.

functions.returns: A string. If either the value is not found, or the found value was not a string, this function returns null.

functions.name: get
functions.desc: Gets and returns a value from the registry.

params.label: valName
params.desc: The name of the value to retreived.

functions.returns: Any type of value, depending on what is associated with the name. This is intended more as a convenience function. Generally you should call getInt, getBool etc dependin on what type you want. It's more descriptive of your intentions.

functions.name: set
functions.desc: Gets and returns a value from the registry.

params.label: valName
params.desc: The name of the value to set.

params.label: value
params.desc: The value to set. This can only be either an int, float or string.

functions.returns: True or false depending on whether the set was a success. It will fail if an invalid value is passed in.

functions.name: clear
functions.desc: Clear all values in the registry.

functions.name: getKeys
functions.desc: Returns an array of all currently tracked key names in the registry.
functions.returns: An array of strings.

scopes.name: _resources
scopes.desc: A namespace to effect resource locations and the Ogre resource system.

functions.name: addResourceLocation
functions.desc: Add a resource location.

params.label: ResPath
params.desc: The path of the resource group.

params.label: String
params.desc: Location type.

params.label: String
params.desc: Resource group name. Defaults to 'General'

functions.name: removeResourceLocation
functions.desc: Remove a resource location.

params.label: String
params.desc: location name.

functions.name: initialiseAllResourceGroups
functions.desc: Initialise all resource groups which have yet to be initialised.

functions.name: initialiseResourceGroup
functions.desc: Initialise a resource group by name.

params.label: String
params.desc: The name of the resource group.

functions.name: prepareResourceGroup
functions.desc: Prepares any resources which are part of a named group.

params.label: String
params.desc: The name of the resource group.

functions.name: destroyResourceGroup
functions.desc: Destroys a resource group, clearing it first, destroying the resources which are part of it, and then removing it from the list of resource groups.

params.label: String
params.desc: The name of the resource group.

functions.name: getResourceGroups
functions.desc: Get a list of all currently defined resource groups.
functions.returns: An array containing strings  of all resource groups.

functions.name: resourceModifiedTime
functions.desc: Get the time as an integer since the given resource was modified.
functions.returns: An integer representing time.

functions.name: parseOgreResourcesFile
functions.desc: Parse an Ogre resources file, adding its resource locations to the system.

params.label: ResPath
params.desc: The path to the resources file to parse.

functions.name: getScriptForParticleSystem
functions.desc: Query a particle system template by name and return the script where it was defined.

params.label: String
params.desc: Name of the particle sytem to query.

functions.returns: The name of the script which contained the template.

functions.name: findGroupContainingResource
functions.desc: Find the group in which a resource exists. Throws an error if the group could not be determined.

params.label: String
params.desc: Name of the resource script to check.

functions.returns: The name of the resource group the script is defined in.

scopes.name: _scene
scopes.desc: A namespace allowing access to the scene.

functions.name: getRootSceneNode
functions.desc: Obtain a handle to the root scene node.
functions.returns: A sceneNode handle.

functions.name: createItem
functions.desc: Create an item, containing a mesh.

params.desc: A string containing a mesh name.
Either _SCENE_STATIC or _SCENE_DYNAMIC. Defaults to _SCENE_DYNAMIC if not provided.

functions.returns: A movableObject userData.

functions.name: createCamera
functions.desc: Create a camera, which can be attached to a scene node to perform rendering.

params.label: String
params.desc: The name of the camera. This must be unique.

functions.returns: A camera object.

functions.name: createLight
functions.desc: Create a light object.
functions.returns: A default light object.

functions.name: createTerrain
functions.desc: Create a terrain object
functions.returns: A terrain object.

functions.name: testRayForSlot
functions.desc: Perform a ray test on the Ogre scene, finding a slot position of the nearest collision.

params.label: Ray
params.desc: A ray object to test with.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: Raycast mask

functions.returns: A SlotPosition if a collision was found. Null if nothing was found.

functions.name: testRayForObject
functions.desc: Perform a ray test on the Ogre scene, returning the first object which is hit.

params.label: Ray
params.desc: A ray object to test with.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: Raycast mask

functions.returns: A movable object. Null if nothing was found.

functions.name: testRayForObjectArray
functions.desc: Perform a ray test on the Ogre scene, returning an array containing all objects which collided.

params.label: Ray
params.desc: A ray object to test with.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: Raycast mask

functions.returns: An array of movable objects. Null if nothing was found.

functions.name: createParticleSystem
functions.desc: Create a particle system movable object.

params.label: String
params.desc: The name of the particle system template name.

functions.returns: A particle system object.

functions.name: removeAllParticleSystemTemplates
functions.desc: Remove all the particle system templates. Useful when reloading resources.

functions.name: createTagPoint
functions.desc: Create a tag point object, which is similar to a SceneNode.
functions.returns: A tag point object.

functions.name: destroyMovableObject
functions.desc: Destroy a movable object.

params.label: MovableObject
params.desc: The movable object to destroy.

functions.name: setAmbientLight
functions.desc: Set the ambient light for the scene.

params.label: ColourValue
params.desc: An integer representing the rgba upper hemisphere colour value.

params.label: ColourValue
params.desc: An integer representing the rgba lower hemisphere colour value.

params.label: Vector3
params.desc: A direction vector representing the hemisphere direction.

functions.name: registerChunkCallback
functions.desc: Register a callback to be called during chunk events.

params.label: Closure
params.desc: The closure which should be called.

functions.name: getNumDataPoints
functions.desc: Get the number of data points in this chunk. This function will throw an error if not called during chunk construction.

functions.name: getDataPointAt
functions.desc:
Fill the provided array with the data point data at index.
The provided array must be a size of at least 4. If not an error will be thrown.
This function will throw an error if not called during chunk construction.
The format of the array will be, position, type, subtype, userdata.

functions.name: insertSceneFile
functions.desc: Load a scene file into the scene. The file will be parsed each time this is called.

params.label: resPath
params.desc: Path to the file to load.

params.label: SceneNode
params.desc: Optional scene node to insert the scene file into. If no node is provided the root node is used instead.

functions.name: parseSceneFile
functions.desc: Parse a scene file as a re-useable object.

params.label: resPath
params.desc: Path to the file to load.

functions.returns: A parsed scene file object.

functions.name: insertParsedSceneFile
functions.desc: Insert a previously parsed scene file.

params.label: ParsedScene
params.desc: The scene object to insert.

params.label: SceneNode
params.desc: Optional scene node to insert the scene file into. If no node is provided the root node is used instead.

functions.name: insertParsedSceneFileGetAnimInfo
functions.desc: Insert a parsed scene file into the scene, returning an anim info object.

params.label: ParsedScene
params.desc: The scene object to insert.

params.label: SceneNode
params.desc: Optional scene node to insert the scene file into. If no node is provided the root node is used instead.

functions.returns: An anim object file.

functions.name: notifyStaticDirty
functions.desc: Notify the scene manager that a static node has changed its transforms, and need to be updated.

params.label: SceneNode
params.desc: The node to update.

scopes.name: _settings
scopes.desc: A namespace to query engine settings.

functions.name: getDataDirectory
functions.returns: A string path to the data directory.

functions.name: getMasterDirectory
functions.returns: A string path to the master directory.

functions.name: getWorldSlotSize
functions.returns: An integer representing the slot size.

functions.name: getCurrentRenderSystem
functions.returns: An integer representing the current render system.

functions.name: getOgreResourcesFile
functions.returns: A string of the absolute path of the OgreResourcesFile. An empty string if the file is not viable.

functions.name: getSaveDirectoryViable
functions.returns: A boolean representing whether or not the save directory is viable.

functions.name: getEngineFeatures
functions.returns: A bitmask integer populated with engine features.

functions.name: getEngineVersion
functions.returns: A table containing engine version entries.

functions.name: getPlatform
functions.returns: An integer representing the platform.

functions.name: getUserSetting
functions.desc: Retreive a user setting. These settings can be specified by the user in the avSetup.cfg file.

params.label: settingName
params.desc: The name of the setting to retreive.

functions.returns: Either a float, int, string or boolean. If a user setting was not found for that name null will be returned.

scopes.name: _settings
scopes.desc: A namespace to query engine settings.

functions.name: getUserSetting
functions.desc: Retreive a user setting. These settings can be specified by the user in the avSetup.cfg file.

params.label: settingName
params.desc: The name of the setting to retreive.

functions.returns: Either a float, int, string or boolean. If a user setting was not found for that name null will be returned.

scopes.name: _slotManager
scopes.desc: Namespace functions relating to the engine's streamable open world.

functions.name: setOrigin
functions.desc: Set the origin of the world. This will trigger and origin shift.

params.label: slotPosition
params.desc: The position to set the origin to.

functions.name: getOrigin
functions.desc: Obtain a slot position representing the origin of the world.

functions.name: setCurrentMap
functions.desc: Set the current map.

params.label: mapName
params.desc: A string representing the target map to switch to.

functions.name: getCurrentMap
functions.returns: Gets the current map as a string.

scopes.name: _state
scopes.desc: Functions relating to the state of the engine.

functions.name: setPauseState
functions.desc: Set the pause state of the engine. The user can selectively pause and start components in the engine using flags.

params.label: flags
params.desc: Bitmask representing the pieces of engine functionality that should be paused.

functions.name: getPauseState
functions.desc: Get the current pause state.
functions.returns: A bitmask representing the current pause state.

functions.name: setDefaultAnimationPauseMask
functions.desc: Set the default pause mask for new animations.

params.label: mask
params.desc: Bitmask representing the default pause mask.

functions.name: getDefaultAnimationPauseMask
functions.desc: Get the default animation pause mask.
functions.returns: A bitmask representing the default animation pause mask.

functions.name: setAnimationPauseMask
functions.desc: Set the global animation pause mask for checking against individual animation masks.

params.label: mask
params.desc: Bitmask representing which animations should be paused.

functions.name: getAnimationPauseMask
functions.desc: Get the global animation pause mask.
functions.returns: A bitmask representing the current animation pause mask.

scopes.name: _system
scopes.desc: Functions relating to the underlying system.

functions.name: mkdir
functions.desc: Create a directory at the specified path.

functions.name: exists
functions.desc: Check if the provided resPath exists on the file system.

functions.name: remove
functions.desc: Remove a file at the provided resPath.

functions.name: removeAll
functions.desc: Recursively remove files and directories.

functions.name: rename
functions.desc: Rename or move a file.

functions.name: createBlankFile
functions.desc: resPath to blank file to be created

functions.name: ensureUserDirectory
functions.desc: Ensure the user directory exists for this project and user:// paths can be used.

functions.name: resolveResPath
functions.desc: Resolve a path which begins with either res:// or user:// to an absolute path

functions.name: getParentPath
functions.desc: Determine the parent path of a given path.

functions.name: getFilenamePath
functions.desc: Return the filename component of a path.

functions.name: getFilesInDirectory
functions.desc: Returns an array of all files in the given directory path.

functions.name: readJSONAsTable
functions.desc: Read a json file, returning the json data as a table object.

functions.name: writeJsonAsFile
functions.desc: Write a table object as a json file.

functions.name: date
functions.desc:
Return the date as a table.
//TODO could do with a proper table.

functions.name: time
functions.desc: Get the current time in seconds since the 1970s epoc.

scopes.name: _timer
scopes.desc: A namespace to trigger time based events.

functions.name: countdown
functions.desc: Begin a countdown timer. When the countdown time is finished the provided context will be called.

params.label: time
params.desc: A number representing the number of milliseconds until the closure should be called.

params.label: closure
params.desc: The target closure to call.

scopes.name: _window
scopes.desc: A namespace to interact with the window system.

functions.name: getSize
functions.returns: The size of the window as a Vec2

functions.name: getActualSize
functions.returns: The actual size of the window as a Vec2. This is used when supporting higher resolution displays.

functions.name: getWidth
functions.returns: The width of the window as an int.

functions.name: getHeight
functions.returns: The height of the window as an int.

functions.name: getActualWidth
functions.returns: The actual width of the window as an int. This value should be used when supporting higher resolution displays.

functions.name: getActualHeight
functions.returns: The actual height of the window as an int. This value should be used when supporting higher resolution displays.

functions.name: getNumDisplays
functions.returns: The number of displays currently available.

functions.name: getPositionX
functions.returns: The X coordinate of the window.

functions.name: getPositionY
functions.returns: The Y coordinate of the window.

functions.name: grabCursor
functions.desc: Set the cursor to be grabbed to the window. If this is enabled it will be made invisible and unable to exit the window. This is useful for FPS style programs.

params.label: grab
params.desc: A boolean value of whether the cursor should be grabbed or not.

functions.name: showCursor
functions.desc: Set the window cursor to either visible or invisible.

params.label: boolean
params.desc: A boolean value of whether the cursor should be visible or not.

functions.name: setCursor
functions.desc: Set the current cursor.

params.label: integer
params.desc: Constant id of the system cursor to use.

functions.name: getRenderTexture
functions.desc: Get the render texture for the window. Can be used to setup compositors.
functions.returns: A texture object representing the window render target.

functions.name: warpMouseInWindow
functions.desc: Warp the mouse to the specified position in the window.

params.label: integer
params.desc: X coordinate in the window.

params.label: integer
params.desc: Y coordinate in the window.

functions.name: setFullscreen
functions.desc: Set the fullscreen mode for when the window is created.

params.label: integer
params.desc: Flag to specify fullscreen. Either _WINDOW_WINDOWED, _WINDOW_FULLSCREEN or _WINDOW_FULLSCREEN_BORDERLESS.

overloads.desc: Set the window to be fullscreen.

params.label: integer
params.desc: Flag to specify fullscreen. Either _WINDOW_WINDOWED, _WINDOW_FULLSCREEN or _WINDOW_FULLSCREEN_BORDERLESS.

functions.name: getFullscreen
functions.desc: Get the fullscreen status of the window.
functions.returns: True or false depending on whether the window is fullscreen.

functions.name: setBorderless
functions.desc: Set the window to be borderless.

params.label: boolean
params.desc: Whether the window should be borderless.

functions.name: getBorderless
functions.desc: Get the borderless state of the window.
functions.returns: True or false depending on whether the window is borderless.

functions.name: getTitle
functions.desc: Get the current title of the window.
functions.returns: The window title as a string.

functions.name: getWindowDisplayIndex
functions.desc: Get the index of the display corresponding to the one on which the window is currently.
functions.returns: The display index as an integer.

functions.name: getDisplayPositionCoordinates
functions.desc: Get the x and y position of the provided display.
functions.returns: Vector2 representing the position.

functions.name: getDisplaySize
functions.desc: Get the width and height of the provided display.
functions.returns: Vector2 representing the position.

functions.name: setTitle
functions.desc: Set the current title of the window.

params.label: string
params.desc: The new title of the window.

functions.name: showMessageBox
functions.desc: Show a modal message box.

params.label: table
params.desc: Table containing data for the message box. //TODO more info please.

functions.name: setPosition
functions.desc: Set the position of the window.

params.label: integer
params.desc: The x position of the window.

params.label: integer
params.desc: The y position of the window.

functions.name: setSize
functions.desc: Set the size of the window.

params.label: integer
params.desc: The width of the window.

params.label: integer
params.desc: The height of the window.

functions.name: toggleRenderStats
functions.desc: Toggle the render stats window

functions.name: getScreenSafeAreaInsets
functions.desc: Get the safe area for the screen. For instance on mobile if a camera notch is present on the device the size of the notch will be included.

scopes.name: _window
scopes.desc: A namespace to interact with the window system.

functions.name: getIntendedWidth
functions.returns: The intended width for the window, which is the width the window will be once setup has been finished.

functions.name: getIntendedHeight
functions.returns: The intended height for the window, which is the height the window will be once setup has been finished.

functions.name: setIntendedWidth
functions.returns: The intended width for the window, which is the width the window will be once setup has been finished.

functions.name: setIntendedHeight
functions.returns: The intended height for the window, which is the height the window will be once setup has been finished.

functions.name: getDisplaySize
functions.desc: Get the width and height of the provided display.
functions.returns: Vector2 representing the position.

scopes.name: _world
scopes.desc: Functions specific to the operation of the world.

functions.name: createWorld
functions.desc: Create the world. This assumes no world exists previously.

functions.name: destroyWorld
functions.desc: Destroy the world. This assumes world exists.

functions.name: getPlayerLoadRadius
functions.returns: Returns the load radius for the world. This defines how many chunks should be loaded within the player radius.

functions.name: upload
functions.desc:
Write data into this buffer, replacing its contents. Unlike mapping,
this can safely be called any number of times per frame. Immutable buffers
(which includes all index buffers) cannot be uploaded to.

params.label: DataBlob
params.desc: The new data. Its size must be a whole number of elements.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: Element to start writing at. Defaults to 0.

params.label: Integer
params.desc:
How many elements to take from the front of the blob.
Defaults to 0, meaning the whole blob. Useful when the blob is a reused
scratch buffer larger than the data currently in it.

functions.name: getNumElements
functions.desc: Number of elements (vertices or indices) this buffer holds.
functions.returns: :Integer

functions.name: getBytesPerElement
functions.desc: Size in bytes of a single element of this buffer.
functions.returns: :Integer

functions.name: setPrimitiveRange
functions.desc:
Limit how much of the buffer is drawn, without re-creating anything.
For an indexed VAO this is the index start and count, otherwise the vertex
start and count. Lets a pooled system (particles, dynamic geometry) draw
only its live elements instead of padding the rest out of sight.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: First element to draw.

params.label: Integer
params.desc: How many elements to draw.

functions.name: getPrimitiveStart
functions.desc: First element currently being drawn.
functions.returns: :Integer

functions.name: getPrimitiveCount
functions.desc: How many elements are currently being drawn.
functions.returns: :Integer

constants.name: _GUI_WIDGET_STATE_HIGHLIGHTED_BUTTON
constants.desc: GUI state highlighted by keyboard or physical input device.

constants.name: _GUI_WIDGET_STATE_HIGHLIGHTED_CURSOR_BUTTON
constants.desc: GUI state highlighted by cursor or button.

constants.name: _GUI_ACTION_HIGHLIGHTED
constants.desc: GUI widget action when focus is gained.

constants.name: _GUI_ACTION_PRESSED
constants.desc: GUI widget action when the widget is pressed. This includes press from keyboard.

constants.name: _GUI_ACTION_RELEASED
constants.desc: GUI wiget action when the widget press ends.

constants.name: _GUI_ACTION_VALUE_CHANGED
constants.desc: GUI wiget action for when the value changes, for instance in a spinner widget.

constants.name: _GUI_INPUT_NONE
constants.desc: Unset input type. Use this if unsetting a gui input map.

constants.name: _GUI_INPUT_PRIMARY
constants.desc: Primary input represents accepting the current input. For instance if a button is highlighted primary input would be selecting it.

constants.name: _TEXT_ALIGN_NATURAL
constants.desc: Align text according to the specified global alignment.

constants.name: _PAUSE_PHYSICS
constants.desc: Pause flag to pause all physics simulations, including dynamics and every collision world.

constants.name: _PAUSE_PHYSICS_DYNAMICS
constants.desc: Pause flag to pause all specifically dynamics.

constants.name: _PAUSE_PHYSICS_COLLISION
constants.desc: Pause flag to pause all specifically collision physics. This pauses all collision worlds.

constants.name: _PAUSE_NAV_MESH
constants.desc: Pause nav mesh calculations and processing.

constants.name: _PAUSE_LIFETIME_COMPONENT
constants.desc: Pause the countdown for entity lifetime components.

constants.name: _PAUSE_ANIMATIONS
constants.desc: Pause all animations currently run by the animation system.

constants.name: _PAUSE_ENTITY_UPDATE
constants.desc: Stop calling the entity update function for entities with a script attached.

constants.name: _PAUSE_SPECIFIC_ANIMATIONS
constants.desc: Pause specific animations based on individual animation pause masks.

constants.name: _GPU_RESIDENCY_ON_STORAGE
constants.desc:
Texture is on storage (i.e. sourced from disk, from listener) A 4x4 blank texture will be shown if user attempts to use this Texture.
No memory is consumed.
While in this state, many settings may not be trusted (width, height, etc) as nothing is loaded.

constants.name: _GPU_RESIDENCY_ON_SYSTEM_RAM
constants.desc:
Texture is on System RAM.
If the texture is fully not resident, a 4x4 blank texture will be shown if user attempts to use this Texture.

constants.name: _GPU_RESIDENCY_RESIDENT
constants.desc: VRAM and other GPU resources have been allocated for this resource.
